# LevelUp Feature Research — 2026-02-21

**Generated by:** 6 parallel web research agents
**Purpose:** Pre-implementation research for Epics 3–8 (39 unbuilt stories)
**Stack constraints:** React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4 + Dexie.js v4 + Zustand v5 + Framer Motion v12, Chrome/Edge 86+ only, no backend, 500 KB gzipped bundle limit, WCAG 2.1 AA+

# Agent 1: Video Player & Content UX

*Covers: transcript panel, Picture-in-Picture, theater mode, VTT parsing, ARIA patterns, performance*

---

## 1. Transcript Panel Best Practices

### Core UX Pattern

The gold-standard pattern — used by YouTube, Coursera, and Microsoft Stream — follows these invariants:

- **Active cue highlighting:** Currently-playing cue gets a background color change. Each cue is clickable to seek.
- **Auto-scroll with user-escape hatch:** Auto-scrolls to keep active cue visible. Suspended on manual scroll. Resumes after 3–5s inactivity via `scrollend` event (Chrome 114+) or `setTimeout` polyfill.
- **Scroll-to-bottom by default** (less jarring than centering).

### Auto-scroll Implementation (No React State for currentTime)

**Critical:** Never put `currentTime` in React state. Triggers re-renders at 4–30 Hz causing 400ms+ latency. Use refs + direct DOM mutation:

```typescript
const handleTimeUpdate = () => {
  const currentTime = videoRef.current!.currentTime;
  const cueIndex = findActiveCueIndex(cues, currentTime); // binary search
  if (cueIndex === activeCueIndexRef.current) return;

  const prev = transcriptRef.current?.querySelector('[data-active="true"]');
  if (prev) prev.removeAttribute('data-active');

  const next = transcriptRef.current?.querySelector(`[data-cue-index="${cueIndex}"]`);
  if (next) {
    next.setAttribute('data-active', 'true');
    activeCueIndexRef.current = cueIndex;
    if (!isUserScrollingRef.current) {
      next.scrollIntoView({
        behavior: prefersReducedMotion ? 'instant' : 'smooth',
        block: 'nearest',
      });
    }
  }
};
```

### ARIA Pattern for Transcript

Do NOT use `aria-live` on the transcript container itself — it fires every 250ms and overwhelms screen readers. Use a separate off-screen live region:

```tsx
{/* Scrollable transcript — NOT a live region */}
<div role="region" aria-label="Video transcript" tabIndex={0} ref={transcriptRef}>
  <p id="transcript-instructions" className="sr-only">
    Click any line to jump to that point in the video.
  </p>
  {cues.map((cue, i) => (
    <button key={cue.id} data-cue-index={i} onClick={() => seekTo(cue.startTime)}
      aria-current={activeCueIndex === i ? 'true' : undefined}
      className="block w-full text-left px-3 py-1 rounded data-[active=true]:bg-blue-100">
      <time className="text-xs text-gray-500 tabular-nums mr-2">{formatTime(cue.startTime)}</time>
      {cue.text}
    </button>
  ))}
</div>

{/* Off-screen announcer — fires only every 5 cues */}
<div aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true" className="sr-only" ref={liveRegionRef} />
```

---

## 2. Picture-in-Picture API Guide

### Two APIs — Know the Difference

- **Video PiP API** (Chrome 70+): Floats only a `<video>` element. No custom HTML controls.
- **Document PiP API** (Chrome 116+): Opens an always-on-top window with arbitrary React-rendered HTML — custom controls, captions, progress bar. **Use this for Story 2-9.**

### Document PiP Implementation

```typescript
const openDocumentPiP = async () => {
  if ('documentPictureInPicture' in window) {
    const pipWindow = await window.documentPictureInPicture.requestWindow({
      width: 480, height: 270,
    });
    // Copy stylesheets into PiP window
    [...document.styleSheets].forEach((sheet) => {
      try {
        const cssRules = [...sheet.cssRules].map((r) => r.cssText).join('');
        const style = pipWindow.document.createElement('style');
        style.textContent = cssRules;
        pipWindow.document.head.appendChild(style);
      } catch {
        const link = pipWindow.document.createElement('link');
        link.rel = 'stylesheet';
        link.href = (sheet as CSSStyleSheet).href!;
        pipWindow.document.head.appendChild(link);
      }
    });
    const container = pipWindow.document.createElement('div');
    pipWindow.document.body.appendChild(container);
    ReactDOM.createRoot(container).render(<MiniPlayer videoRef={videoRef} />);
    pipWindow.addEventListener('pagehide', () => setPiPMode(false));
  } else {
    // Fallback to standard video PiP
    await videoRef.current?.requestPictureInPicture();
  }
};
```

### TypeScript Declaration (not yet in lib.dom.d.ts)

```typescript
interface DocumentPictureInPicture extends EventTarget {
  requestWindow(options?: { width?: number; height?: number }): Promise<Window>;
  readonly window: Window | null;
}
declare global {
  interface Window { documentPictureInPicture?: DocumentPictureInPicture; }
}
```

**Key gotchas:**
- `requestPictureInPicture()` must be called from a synchronous user gesture (click) — never in `useEffect` or `setTimeout`.
- Document PiP CSS is isolated — must copy stylesheets explicitly.
- `resize` event is on the `PictureInPictureWindow` object, not on `document`.

---

## 3. Theater Mode Implementation

```tsx
<div
  data-theater={isTheaterMode}
  className={cn(
    "relative rounded-[24px] overflow-hidden transition-all duration-300",
    "data-[theater=true]:fixed data-[theater=true]:inset-0 data-[theater=true]:z-50",
    "data-[theater=true]:rounded-none data-[theater=true]:w-screen data-[theater=true]:h-screen"
  )}
>
  <video ref={videoRef} className="w-full h-full object-contain bg-black" />
  <VideoControls />
</div>

{isTheaterMode && (
  <div className="fixed inset-0 z-40 bg-black/80 transition-opacity duration-300"
    onClick={() => setTheaterMode(false)} aria-hidden="true" />
)}
```

Use `IntersectionObserver` (threshold: 0.5) to auto-trigger PiP offer when video scrolls out of view. Theater and PiP are mutually exclusive — enforce in Zustand store.

**Keyboard shortcut:** `t` key (YouTube convention). Manage focus: on open move focus to controls, on close return to the theater button.

---

## 4. VTT Parser Recommendation

**Option A: `@plussub/srt-vtt-parser`** (~2–4 KB gzipped)
- TypeScript, zero dependencies, handles SRT + VTT, returns times in ms
- Recommended for production use

**Option B: DIY 30-line parser** (zero bundle cost)
- Handle these edge cases: UTF-8 BOM (`ï»¿WEBVTT`), CRLF normalization, MM:SS vs HH:MM:SS timestamps, NOTE/STYLE block skipping, overlapping cues (use `findLast` not `findIndex`).

**Option C: Browser native `<track>` element**
- Zero bundle cost, CORS restrictions apply, less ergonomic API.

---

## 5. ARIA Media Player Patterns

### Complete Keyboard Map

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `Space` / `k` | Play/Pause |
| `m` | Mute toggle |
| `f` | Fullscreen |
| `t` | Theater mode |
| `ArrowRight` | +5s |
| `ArrowLeft` | -5s |
| `ArrowUp` | Volume +10% |
| `ArrowDown` | Volume -10% |
| `Home` / `End` | Seek start/end |
| `1`–`9` | Jump to 10%–90% |
| `c` | Toggle captions |

### Seekbar — Use `<input type="range">`

The W3C APG recommends native `<input type="range">` over custom `role="slider"` for better AT compatibility:

```tsx
<input type="range" min={0} max={duration} value={currentTime} step={1}
  aria-label="Playback position"
  aria-valuetext={`${formatTimeText(currentTime)} of ${formatTimeText(duration)}`}
  className="w-full accent-blue-600"
/>
```

`aria-valuetext` converts raw seconds to human-readable time — critical for screen reader users.

### Full Player ARIA Structure

- Player wrapper: `role="region"` + `aria-label="Video player"`
- Controls: `role="toolbar"` + `aria-label="Video controls"`
- Play/Pause: `aria-pressed={isPlaying}` + dynamic `aria-label`
- PiP button: `disabled={!isPiPSupported}`
- Chapter markers: `aria-label="Chapter: {title} at {time}"`

**Avoid `role="application"`** — it suppresses all screen reader keyboard shortcuts.

---

## 6. Performance Patterns

### Progress Bar Updates

Use `transform: scaleX()` (GPU-composited), not `width: X%` (triggers layout):

```css
.progress-fill {
  transform-origin: left center;
  transform: scaleX(0);
  will-change: transform;
  transition: none; /* disable during drag */
}
```

Update via `timeupdate` event (~250ms interval) for normal playback. Use rAF loop only during pointer drag for frame-accurate scrubbing. Listen to `seeked` event to finalize state after seeks.

### `prefers-reduced-motion` Hook

```typescript
export const usePrefersReducedMotion = () => {
  const [value, setValue] = useState(() => !window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)').matches);
  useEffect(() => {
    const mql = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)');
    const listener = (e: MediaQueryListEvent) => setValue(!e.matches);
    mql.addEventListener('change', listener);
    return () => mql.removeEventListener('change', listener);
  }, []);
  return value;
};
```

Apply to transcript scroll (`'instant'` vs `'smooth'`), theater mode transition duration, and confetti.

---

# Agent 2: Note-Taking System & Markdown Editor

*Covers: editor library selection, custom protocol security, canvas capture, resizable panels, autosave, timestamp UX, PDF export*

---

## 1. Markdown Editor Comparison

| Library | Gzipped Bundle | TypeScript | Custom Renderer | Accessibility | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `@uiw/react-md-editor` | ~65–80 KB | Excellent | Yes — rehype pipeline | Moderate | **Recommended** |
| CodeMirror 6 + `react-markdown` | ~158 KB total | Excellent | Split-pane only | Strong | Too large for budget |
| Tiptap | ~65–70 KB | Excellent | Yes — Node views | Poor | Fails WCAG requirement |
| Milkdown | ~200+ KB total | Good | Yes — headless | Moderate | Too complex, too large |

**Use `@uiw/react-md-editor`.** Integrates directly into remark/rehype for custom protocol links, ships split-pane out of the box, leaves room for other Epic 3 deps in the 500 KB budget.

**Do not use `@react-pdf/renderer`** — it is ~392 KB gzipped alone (78% of total budget).

### Performance Fix: Debounce the Preview

```typescript
const [editorValue, setEditorValue] = useState(note.content);
const [previewValue, setPreviewValue] = useState(note.content);
const debouncedPreview = useDebouncedCallback((val) => setPreviewValue(val), 300);

// On editor change:
setEditorValue(val);      // immediate — editor stays responsive
debouncedPreview(val);    // delayed — remark/rehype pipeline doesn't run every keystroke
autosave(val);
```

Memoize the `commands` prop — it creates a new array reference every render otherwise.

---

## 2. Custom Protocol Security Guide

### Recommended: `rehype-sanitize` in the remark pipeline

```typescript
import rehypeSanitize, { defaultSchema } from 'rehype-sanitize';

const noteSchema = {
  ...defaultSchema,
  protocols: {
    ...defaultSchema.protocols,
    href: [...(defaultSchema.protocols?.href ?? []), 'video', 'screenshot'],
  },
};

<MDEditor.Markdown
  source={content}
  rehypePlugins={[[rehypeSanitize, noteSchema]]}
  components={{ a: CustomLink }}
/>
```

### Custom Link Renderer

```tsx
function CustomLink({ href, children }: LinkProps) {
  if (href?.startsWith('video://')) {
    const url = new URL(href);
    const t = Number(url.searchParams.get('t') ?? 0);
    return (
      <button onClick={() => seekVideo(t)}
        className="inline-flex items-center gap-1 bg-blue-50 text-blue-700 border border-blue-200 rounded px-1.5 py-0.5 text-xs font-mono hover:bg-blue-100 focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-blue-500"
        aria-label={`Jump to ${formatTimestamp(t)}`}>
        <Clock className="w-3 h-3" aria-hidden="true" />
        {formatTimestamp(t)}
      </button>
    );
  }
  if (href?.startsWith('screenshot://')) {
    return <ScreenshotThumbnail noteId={href.replace('screenshot://', '')} />;
  }
  return <a href={href} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">{children}</a>;
}
```

---

## 3. Video Frame Capture Implementation

```typescript
async function captureVideoFrame(videoEl: HTMLVideoElement, noteId: string) {
  const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
  canvas.width = videoEl.videoWidth;
  canvas.height = videoEl.videoHeight;
  canvas.getContext('2d')!.drawImage(videoEl, 0, 0);

  const blob = await new Promise<Blob>((res, rej) =>
    canvas.toBlob((b) => b ? res(b) : rej(new Error('toBlob failed')), 'image/jpeg', 0.85)
  );

  // Small thumbnail for display (no Dexie indexing)
  const thumbCanvas = document.createElement('canvas');
  thumbCanvas.width = 200;
  thumbCanvas.height = Math.round((videoEl.videoHeight / videoEl.videoWidth) * 200);
  thumbCanvas.getContext('2d')!.drawImage(videoEl, 0, 0, 200, thumbCanvas.height);
  const thumbnailBase64 = thumbCanvas.toDataURL('image/jpeg', 0.7);

  return { blob, thumbnailBase64, capturedAt: Date.now(), videoTimestamp: videoEl.currentTime, noteId };
}
```

**CORS gotcha:** `crossOrigin="anonymous"` must be set on `<video>` BEFORE the `src` attribute. Order matters in JSX (the HTML attribute order differs from the DOM property order — React sets `crossOrigin` property correctly regardless of JSX order, but test this).

**Dexie schema:** Never index `imageBlob` or `thumbnailBase64` fields. List only indexed fields in `.stores()`:
```typescript
screenshots: '++id, noteId, capturedAt, videoTimestamp'  // no blob fields
```

---

## 4. Resizable Panel Recommendation

**Use `react-resizable-panels`** (~6–8 KB gzipped) — already powers shadcn/ui's `<Resizable>` component.

- Implements WAI-ARIA Window Splitter pattern (`role="separator"`, `aria-valuenow/min/max`)
- Keyboard resizing: `ArrowLeft/Right`, `Home/End`, `Enter` to toggle collapse
- `autoSaveId` for localStorage persistence, or pass a custom storage adapter for Dexie

Do NOT use CSS `resize` — fails WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1 (no keyboard support).

---

## 5. Autosave Pattern Guide

**Use `use-debounce` (~1 KB gzipped) with `maxWait`:**

```typescript
const debouncedSave = useDebouncedCallback(
  async (content: string) => {
    await db.notes.update(noteId, { content, updatedAt: Date.now() });
  },
  1000,      // debounce: save 1s after user stops typing
  { maxWait: 5000 }  // throttle guarantee: always saves within 5s of first change
);
```

**Optimistic updates:** Update Zustand store immediately, persist to Dexie asynchronously. Never wait for Dexie before updating the UI.

**Multi-tab sync:** Use `BroadcastChannel` API to invalidate other tabs' note caches when a note is saved.

**Save indicator:** `aria-live="polite"` on the save status span.

---

## 6. Timestamp UX Patterns

**Keyboard shortcut:** `Ctrl+Shift+T` / `Cmd+Shift+T` — inserts `[0:45](video://courseId/lessonId?t=45)` at cursor, shows "Timestamp added" toast.

**Timeline markers on progress bar:** Render small clickable `<button>` dots at `(timestamp / duration) * 100%` left positions. Include `aria-label` with note preview text.

```typescript
function formatTimestamp(seconds: number): string {
  const h = Math.floor(seconds / 3600);
  const m = Math.floor((seconds % 3600) / 60);
  const s = Math.floor(seconds % 60);
  return h > 0
    ? `${h}:${String(m).padStart(2, '0')}:${String(s).padStart(2, '0')}`
    : `${m}:${String(s).padStart(2, '0')}`;
}
```

---

## 7. Export Options Comparison

| Library | Gzip | Text Selectable | Quality | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `@react-pdf/renderer` | ~392 KB | Yes | Excellent | DISQUALIFIED |
| `jsPDF` alone | ~120 KB | Yes | Moderate | Viable |
| `html2canvas` + `jsPDF` | ~200 KB | No | Good | Acceptable lazy-loaded |
| `react-to-print` + `window.print()` | ~3 KB | Yes (native) | Excellent | **Recommended** |

`react-to-print` triggers `window.print()` on a target component with print CSS. Native PDF with selectable text and proper fonts, no third-party PDF engine.

**Markdown export:** Use File System Access API (Chrome 86+) with a `data:` URI download fallback.

---

## 8. Critical Gotchas

1. **IndexedDB eviction:** Call `navigator.storage.persist()` on app init. Catch `QuotaExceededError` and delete oldest screenshots to free space.
2. **Canvas CORS taint is permanent** per video session — if `crossOrigin` was not set before `src`, you must recreate the video element.
3. **`@uiw/react-md-editor` preview re-renders on every keystroke** — debounce the preview value separately from the editor value.
4. **MiniSearch index is in-memory only** — re-index from Dexie on page load, or serialize/deserialize the index to Dexie for faster startup.
5. **`window.print()` requires all async content (screenshots) to be resolved BEFORE calling** — use `Promise.all` to pre-load blobs.
6. **Zustand `persist` middleware drops `Blob` values** via `JSON.stringify` — never put blobs in Zustand, only Dexie record IDs.

---

---

# Agent 3: Gamification, Streaks & Behavioral Psychology

*Covers: streak mechanics, heatmap libraries, timezone handling, badge design, confetti, notification UX, challenge design, streak protection*

---

## 1. Streak Psychology Research Summary

### Core Mechanism
Streaks exploit **loss aversion** — users respond more to threat of losing a streak than to the reward of extending it. Loss-framed messaging outperforms gain-framed messaging.

### Key Data Points
- **7-day threshold:** Users who maintain streaks 7 days are **3.6x more likely** to stay engaged long-term. This is the most important milestone.
- **Streak Freeze impact:** Duolingo's freeze mechanic increased DAU by +0.38%.
- **Habit formation:** 59–154 days (PMC 2024 meta-analysis) — streaks are scaffolding for the first phase, not a permanent solution.

### Optimal Minimum Activity Threshold
**5–10 minutes, or 1 completed lesson.** Do NOT require completing a full chapter — this triggers abstinence violation on hard days.

### Danger Zones

| Stage | Risk |
|---|---|
| Day 1–7 | Abandonment — streak too short to feel valuable |
| Day 21–60 | Anxiety activation — dreads missing a day |
| Day 60+ | Hollow maintenance — minimum effort to preserve number |
| Post-break | Abstinence violation — quits entirely after one miss |

**The 80% Rule:** Surface "consistency rate" (24/30 days) alongside streak count to reduce anxiety.

---

## 2. Heatmap Library Comparison

| Library | Gzip (est.) | TypeScript | A11y | Maintained | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `react-activity-calendar` | ~25–30 KB | Yes (90%) | Respects reduced-motion, configurable colors | Active (v3.1.1 2024) | **Recommended** |
| `@uiw/react-heat-map` | Unknown | Yes | Less robust | Moderate | Fallback |
| `react-calendar-heatmap` | Unknown | Via `@types` | None | Abandoned (2021) | Avoid |
| Custom SVG | 0 KB | N/A | Full control | N/A | If budget critical |

**v3 breaking change:** `react-activity-calendar` v3 is pure ESM, named exports only.

### Colorblind-Safe Palette (Replaces GitHub's green)

```typescript
const colorBlindSafeScale = [
  '#eee',    // level 0 — no activity
  '#b3cde3', // level 1 — light blue
  '#6baed6', // level 2 — medium blue
  '#2171b5', // level 3 — strong blue
  '#084594', // level 4 — maximum activity
];

<ActivityCalendar data={data} theme={{ light: colorBlindSafeScale }} />
```

Add text labels to the legend — never convey status through color alone.

---

## 3. Timezone Handling Strategy

**Always store timestamps as UTC ISO 8601. Always store the IANA timezone with each activity record.**

```typescript
// CORRECT
const activityRecord = {
  id: crypto.randomUUID(),
  timestampUTC: new Date().toISOString(),  // "2026-02-21T15:30:00.000Z"
  userTimezone: Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone,
};

// WRONG — never store ambiguous local date strings
const activityRecord = { date: '2026-02-21' };
```

### Day Boundary Calculation

```typescript
function getLocalDateString(utcTimestamp: string, timezone: string): string {
  return new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-CA', {
    timeZone: timezone, year: 'numeric', month: '2-digit', day: '2-digit',
  }).format(new Date(utcTimestamp)); // returns "2026-02-21"
}
```

### Critical Date Arithmetic Rules

- **Never use `new Date('2026-02-21')`** — parsed as UTC midnight, not local midnight.
- **Never use `timestamp + 86400000`** for "next day" — breaks on DST transitions. Use `date.setDate(date.getDate() + 1)`.
- **Never use `toLocaleDateString()`** — non-deterministic across locales.

### Grace Period: 3 hours past midnight

Check if local hour < 3 → yesterday still counts for streak continuation.

---

## 4. Achievement Badge Psychology

### Earned vs Cheap

**Earned:** Requires multi-session effort, has visible progress state, rarity communicated, badge icon reflects the achievement.

**Cheap (avoid):** Awarded for account creation, automatic without user action, more than 2 badges per session.

### Tiered System (Prevents Badge Inflation)

| Tier | Frequency | Criteria Example |
|---|---|---|
| Bronze | Days 1–7: 2–3 total | First lesson, first quiz, 3-day streak |
| Silver | Days 8–30: 3–4 total | 7-day streak, first course completed |
| Gold | Ongoing: 1 per major milestone | 30-day streak, 5 courses |
| Platinum | 1–2 per quarter | 100-day streak |

### Badge Unlock Animation (Framer Motion)

```typescript
const badgeVariants = {
  hidden: { scale: 0, opacity: 0, rotate: -15 },
  visible: {
    scale: 1, opacity: 1, rotate: 0,
    transition: { type: 'spring', stiffness: 400, damping: 20 },
  },
};

function BadgeUnlock({ badge }: { badge: Badge }) {
  const shouldReduceMotion = useReducedMotion();
  return (
    <motion.div variants={shouldReduceMotion ? {} : badgeVariants}
      initial="hidden" animate="visible">
      <img src={badge.iconUrl} alt={badge.name} />
    </motion.div>
  );
}
```

**Static `prefers-reduced-motion` fallback:** Fade in (`opacity: 0 → 1`, 200ms). Never skip animation entirely.

**Modal vs toast:** Silver+ = modal. Bronze = toast. Never show a full modal for minor badges.

---

## 5. Confetti/Celebration Implementation

**Use `canvas-confetti`** (~4 KB gzipped) — Canvas API, off-main-thread via `useWorker: true`. Do NOT use `react-confetti` (DOM-based, thrashes React reconciler).

```typescript
import confetti from 'canvas-confetti';
import { useReducedMotion } from 'framer-motion';

function useCelebration() {
  const shouldReduceMotion = useReducedMotion();

  const triggerStreakCelebration = useCallback(() => {
    if (shouldReduceMotion) return;
    confetti({
      particleCount: 80, spread: 70, origin: { y: 0.6 },
      colors: ['#2563eb', '#7c3aed', '#d97706', '#059669'],
      disableForReducedMotion: true, useWorker: true,
    });
  }, [shouldReduceMotion]);

  return { triggerStreakCelebration };
}
```

### Proportionality Guide

| Event | Celebration |
|---|---|
| Lesson completed | Badge toast only |
| 7-day streak | Light confetti burst |
| Badge (bronze) | Toast |
| Badge (silver/gold) | Modal + confetti |
| Course completed | Maximum confetti + modal |

---

## 6. Notification Permission UX Guide

**Never call `Notification.requestPermission()` on page load.** Chrome Lighthouse flags this as a best-practices violation.

### "Soft Ask" Pattern: Custom dialog BEFORE browser dialog

```typescript
function NotificationPermissionPrompt({ onClose }: { onClose: () => void }) {
  const handleEnable = async () => {
    onClose();
    const permission = await Notification.requestPermission();
    if (permission === 'granted') scheduleStudyReminders();
  };
  return (
    <Dialog>
      <DialogContent>
        <h2>Never break your streak</h2>
        <p>Get a gentle reminder 30 minutes before your usual study time.</p>
        <Button onClick={handleEnable}>Enable reminders</Button>
        <Button variant="ghost" onClick={onClose}>Not now</Button>
      </DialogContent>
    </Dialog>
  );
}
```

**Timing triggers:** After 3-day streak, after setting a study goal, after 3 separate visits. Never on first visit or mid-task.

**If denied:** Surface in-app toast reminders (no permission required), "Add to Home Screen" prompt.

---

## 7. Challenge Design Patterns

**7-day challenges are the primary format** — brief interventions produce effect sizes of 1.57 vs 0.30 for 20-week programs (JMIR Serious Games 2024).

### 7-Day Sprint Structure
- Days 1: Easy entry
- Days 2–5: Progressive difficulty
- Day 6: Review/quiz
- Day 7: Capstone assessment
- Micro-milestones at day 3 and day 5

### 30-Day Challenge Scaffolding (4 weekly phases, each with its own milestone badge, allow 2 missed days without penalty)

### Progress Visualization: Calendar Grid

```tsx
function ChallengeCalendar({ totalDays, completedDays }: Props) {
  return (
    <div role="list" aria-label={`${completedDays} of ${totalDays} days completed`}>
      {Array.from({ length: totalDays }, (_, i) => {
        const status = i < completedDays ? 'completed' : i === completedDays ? 'current' : 'future';
        return (
          <div key={i} role="listitem" aria-label={`Day ${i + 1}: ${status}`}
            className={cn(
              'w-8 h-8 rounded-full',
              status === 'completed' && 'bg-blue-600',
              status === 'current' && 'border-2 border-blue-600 animate-pulse',
              status === 'future' && 'border border-gray-300',
            )} />
        );
      })}
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

## 8. Streak Protection Mechanics

### Recommended Freeze Token System

| Source | Amount |
|---|---|
| Reach 7-day streak | +1 |
| Complete a challenge | +1 |
| 30-day streak milestone | +2 |
| 100-day streak milestone | +3 |
| Maximum holdable | 5 |

**Auto-apply freeze:** When user misses a day and has tokens, consume one token automatically (no manual activation required).

**One-time monthly grace restore:** If no tokens remain, allow one free restore per 30-day window.

**Multi-day gaps:** No protection — streak resets. Only protect single missed days.

### Dexie Schema

```typescript
interface ActivityRecord {
  id: string;           // UUID
  timestampUTC: string; // indexed
  userTimezone: string;
  courseId: string;     // indexed
  durationSeconds: number;
  activityType: 'video' | 'quiz' | 'reading';
}
// schema: 'id, timestampUTC, courseId'

interface StreakState {
  currentStreak: number;
  longestStreak: number;
  freezeTokens: number;         // 0–5
  lastActivityDateLocal: string; // display only
  manualRestoreUsedThisMonth: boolean;
}
```

### Critical Gotchas

1. Use Dexie transactions for streak state updates — multi-tab writes can race-condition.
2. Mirror critical state (streak count, last date, tokens) to `localStorage` as IndexedDB eviction backup.
3. Show longest-streak alongside current — prevents abandonment after a break.
4. Show freeze/restore option BEFORE the user leaves after a break. Never send guilt notifications after a streak has already broken.
5. Empty heatmap looks broken — show the last 4 weeks only until there's enough data to fill the full year grid.

---

---

# Agent 4: Learning Intelligence & Momentum Algorithms

*Covers: "Continue Learning" algorithm, zero-decision UX, momentum scoring, abandonment detection, learning velocity, spaced repetition, weekly insights*

---

## 1. "Continue Learning" Algorithm Design

### Client-Side Scoring Algorithm

```typescript
function continueLearningScore(course: CourseProgress): number {
  const daysSinceAccess = (Date.now() - course.lastAccessedAt) / 86400000;

  // Recency: exponential decay with 7-day half-life
  const recencyScore = 100 * Math.pow(0.5, daysSinceAccess / 7);

  // Completion: sweet spot 20-80% (urgency boost near done)
  const pct = course.completionPercent;
  const completionScore = pct >= 80 ? 90 : pct >= 20 ? 70 : pct >= 5 ? 40 : 10;

  // Session frequency: normalize to 0–100 (cap at 20 sessions)
  const frequencyScore = Math.min(100, (course.sessionCount / 20) * 100);

  return (0.40 * recencyScore) + (0.30 * completionScore) + (0.30 * frequencyScore);
}
```

**Weight validation:** The 40/30/30 split is well-grounded in recommendation systems research (UCSD Time-Weight Collaborative Filtering). Recency being highest-weighted aligns with all major platform implementations.

**Half-life:** 7 days is right for a learning app. Netflix-scale uses longer half-lives; educational content has shorter relevance windows.

### Selection Rules

- Select top-scoring course where `completionPercent > 0 && completionPercent < 100`
- Score tie (within 5 points) → prefer higher `completionPercent` (urgency)
- All courses at 0% → surface first enrolled course with CTA "Start your first lesson"
- All courses at 100% → hide "Continue" card, show "Explore New Courses"
- Single course enrolled → always show it

---

## 2. Zero-Decision UX Patterns

**One card, one action.** Never two cards or a list.

**Show enough context to feel confident, not enough to cause reconsideration:**
- "Continue Chapter 3" (good) vs "Resume Course" (weak)
- "Pick up where you left off — Chapter 3: Data Types" (strong)
- Never show: "You last studied 4 days ago" (guilt-inducing)

**The Headspace Pattern:** Primary CTA = "Continue Chapter X". Secondary option (collapsed) = "Browse all chapters."

**Do NOT show:**
- "Which course do you want to continue?" — defeats the algorithm's purpose
- "Based on your activity, we think you might want to..." — sounds apologetic

---

## 3. Momentum Scoring System

### Hot / Warm / Cold Thresholds

| Classification | Score | Days Since Access |
|---|---|---|
| Hot | 70–100 | 0–3 days |
| Warm | 35–69 | 4–14 days |
| Cold | 1–34 | 15–30 days |
| Inactive | 0 | 30+ days |

**Display:** Use "Active / Slowing / Paused" instead of "Hot/Cold" — less emotionally loaded, more screen-reader friendly. Color cues (green/amber/grey) paired with text labels.

**Do not show momentum badges on courses with fewer than 3 sessions** — label as "New" instead.

---

## 4. Abandonment Detection Signals

### Most Predictive Behavioral Signals (PMC Systematic Review, 21 MOOC studies)

1. **First-week inactivity** — strongest single predictor
2. **Video non-completion pattern** — habitual early exit in first 2 sessions
3. **Quiz/assessment avoidance** — completing readings but skipping quizzes
4. **Session shortening trend** — 25min → 15min → 8min across visits
5. **Login frequency decline** — first week of reduced login is optimal intervention window

```typescript
function abandonmentRisk(signals: AbandonmentSignals): 'low' | 'medium' | 'high' {
  let riskPoints = 0;
  if (signals.daysSinceLastAccess > 14) riskPoints += 3;
  else if (signals.daysSinceLastAccess > 7) riskPoints += 2;
  if (signals.sessionCount <= 1 && signals.daysSinceEnrollment > 3) riskPoints += 2;
  if (signals.sessionDurationTrend === 'decreasing') riskPoints += 2;
  if (signals.quizCompletionRate < 0.3) riskPoints += 1;
  return riskPoints >= 5 ? 'high' : riskPoints >= 3 ? 'medium' : 'low';
}
```

### Intervention Copy Guidelines

**Approved patterns:**
- "Chapter 4 is waiting for you — you were on a roll." (progress anchor)
- "Quick win available: your next quiz is just 5 questions." (minimal action)
- "You've already put in the hard work. 15 more minutes gets you to Chapter 5." (investment framing)

**Prohibited patterns:**
- "You haven't studied in X days" (guilt)
- "Don't break your streak!" (shame as motivation)
- "Are you still interested in this course?" (passive-aggressive)
- "You're falling behind your goal" (deficit framing)

---

## 5. Learning Velocity Definition

**Operational definition for LevelUp:**
```
Learning velocity = content units completed / active study time
```

Display as **"X chapters per week"** (not hours — too granular).

| Velocity | Label |
|---|---|
| 0 units/week | Inactive |
| 1–2 units/week | Steady |
| 3–5 units/week | Strong |
| 6+ units/week | High |

**Idle timeout:** Pause session timer after 5 minutes of no interaction. Use `document.visibilityState` to pause when tab is backgrounded.

**Display:** Show sparkline of weekly velocity over 6 weeks. Never show declining velocity prominently — frame as "last week was lighter."

---

## 6. Spaced Repetition Evaluation

**Recommendation: Defer to a future epic.**

Rationale: Epic 6 tracks passive consumption. Spaced repetition requires active flashcard generation — a different behavior loop belonging in a "Notes & Review" epic.

**If forced to include:** Implement minimal SM-2 in ~50 lines of TypeScript (zero bundle cost) rather than adding `ts-fsrs`. FSRS v5 is superior to SM-2 (20–30% fewer reviews for same retention), but the complexity cost isn't worth it in Epic 6 scope.

---

## 7. Weekly Insights Design

### Top 5 Metrics to Surface (Research-Backed)

1. Time studied this week (total active minutes)
2. Chapters/videos completed (concrete progress unit)
3. Streak (days this week, not all-time — reduces shame)
4. Most active course (personalization hook)
5. Velocity trend ("Up from last week" / "A lighter week")

**Never include:** Comparison to other users, total idle time, exact completion percentages for all courses.

### Positive Framing by Week Type

| Week Type | Headline |
|---|---|
| High activity | "Strong week — 4 chapters done" |
| Average | "Good progress on TypeScript. Next week: Chapter 6" |
| Low activity | "A quieter week — that's okay. Your next chapter is ready" |
| Zero activity | "Course progress saved. Pick up where you left off any time" |

**Empty state:** Show friendly illustration + progress anchor ("You're 47% through…") + one specific action ("Your next lesson is Chapter 4 — 18 minutes"). Never blank, never a warning.

### Cold-Start Problem

- Require minimum 2 sessions before displaying momentum classifications
- Always handle `sessionCount === 0` explicitly — algorithm must not return null/undefined
- Do not show "Hot/Warm/Cold" on courses with < 3 sessions

---

---

# Agent 5: Analytics Dashboard & Learning Metrics

*Covers: Recharts best practices, Dexie.js aggregation, learning metrics prioritization, forecasting, empty states, chart accessibility, CSV export, dashboard layout*

---

## 1. Recharts Best Practices

### ResponsiveContainer

```tsx
{/* Always wrap in a div with explicit pixel height */}
<div style={{ width: '100%', height: 300 }}>
  <ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height="100%">
    <LineChart data={data}>...</LineChart>
  </ResponsiveContainer>
</div>
```

**Infinite resize loop fix:** Add `overflow: hidden` on the parent div (not the `width="99%"` workaround). Or `min-width: 0` on a flex child.

**`isAnimationActive` must be set on the data component, not the chart element:**
```tsx
<Line isAnimationActive={false} dataKey="minutes" />  // correct
```

**For frequently-updating data:** Always use `isAnimationActive={false}` — prevents "never finishes rendering" state.

**Respect `prefers-reduced-motion`:**
```tsx
const prefersReducedMotion = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches;
<Line isAnimationActive={!prefersReducedMotion} animationDuration={600} />
```

### Custom Tooltip Pattern

```tsx
const CustomTooltip = ({ active, payload, label }: CustomTooltipProps) => {
  if (!active || !payload?.length) return null;
  return (
    <div className="bg-white border border-gray-200 rounded-xl px-3 py-2 shadow-md text-sm">
      <p className="font-semibold text-gray-700 mb-1">{label}</p>
      {payload.map((entry) => (
        <p key={entry.name} style={{ color: entry.color }}>
          {entry.name}: {entry.value} min
        </p>
      ))}
    </div>
  );
};
```

**Always guard `if (!active || !payload?.length) return null`** — failing causes empty tooltip container on first render.

### Performance with Large Datasets

- **Never pass raw session records to charts.** Group to daily/weekly buckets first.
- `const chartData = useMemo(() => buildChartData(sessions), [sessions])` — single most impactful optimization.
- Wrap chart component in `React.memo`.
- For 365-day yearly trends: use LTTB (Largest Triangle Three Buckets) downsampling to ~90 points.

### v3 Migration Notes

Stay on v2 unless there is a blocking bug. Key v3 changes: `TooltipContentProps` replaces `TooltipProps`, Z-index now depends on render order (move `<Tooltip>` below `<Legend>`), accessibility enabled by default (verify no ARIA conflicts).

---

## 2. Dexie.js Aggregation Strategy

### No Native GROUP BY — Aggregate in JavaScript

```typescript
// Always store timestamps as numbers (Date.getTime()), not Date objects or strings
// schema: 'id, startedAt, courseId'
// Use: .where('startedAt').between(from.getTime(), to.getTime(), true, true)
```

**Compound index gotcha:** `.between()` with compound index requires array: `.between([courseId, from], [courseId, to])`.

### Grouping by Day

```typescript
function groupByDay(sessions: StudySession[]): DailyBucket[] {
  const buckets = new Map<string, DailyBucket>();
  for (const s of sessions) {
    const key = new Date(s.startedAt).toISOString().slice(0, 10); // 'YYYY-MM-DD'
    const existing = buckets.get(key);
    if (existing) {
      existing.totalMinutes += s.durationMinutes;
      existing.sessionCount += 1;
    } else {
      buckets.set(key, { date: key, totalMinutes: s.durationMinutes, sessionCount: 1 });
    }
  }
  return Array.from(buckets.values()).sort((a, b) => a.date.localeCompare(b.date));
}
```

### Performance Ceiling

- 500 records, indexed range query: ~2–8ms ✓
- 5,000 records: ~20–50ms ✓ (acceptable)
- 50,000 records: ~200–500ms (add pre-computed cache table)

**For Epic 7 (500 sessions):** Raw queries are fine. Add `useMemo` in Zustand selector. No cache table needed yet.

**Use `useLiveQuery` from `dexie-react-hooks`** for real-time chart updates when a session completes.

---

## 3. Learning Metrics Prioritization

### Tier 1 (Most Meaningful — Research Backed)

**Study consistency (streak / active days per week)** — strongest behavioral predictor of completion. Lead with this.

**Active days count, not total hours.** 15 min/day × 7 days = better retention than 2 hours/Sunday.

**Course completion rate** — lagging indicator that validates all other metrics.

### Tier 2 (Meaningful Context)

- Learning velocity trend (week-over-week)
- Subject/topic distribution
- Average session duration

### Tier 3 (Vanity — Handle Carefully)

- Lifetime total hours (feels impressive, poor outcome correlation)
- Total session count (low-quality sessions inflate this)
- Login frequency (can reflect cramming, not learning)

### Dashboard Ordering

1. Current streak + best streak (emotional hook)
2. Days active this week/month
3. Study time this week
4. Week-over-week velocity change
5. Course completion rates
6. Subject distribution
7. Lifetime hours (milestone celebration only)

---

## 4. Progress Forecasting Implementation

```typescript
function forecastCompletionDate(
  totalCourseMinutes: number,
  minutesCompleted: number,
  weeklyHistory: WeeklyDataPoint[]
): { estimatedDate: Date; confidence: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' } {
  // Exponential weights — most recent week highest weight
  const weights = weeklyHistory.map((_, i) => Math.pow(2, i));
  const totalWeight = weights.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
  const weightedAvgPerWeek = weeklyHistory.reduce((sum, w, i) =>
    sum + (w.minutesStudied * weights[i]) / totalWeight, 0);

  const minutesRemaining = totalCourseMinutes - minutesCompleted;
  const weeksRemaining = minutesRemaining / (weightedAvgPerWeek || 1);
  const estimatedDate = new Date();
  estimatedDate.setDate(estimatedDate.getDate() + weeksRemaining * 7);

  const variance = weeklyHistory.reduce((sum, w) =>
    sum + Math.pow(w.minutesStudied - weightedAvgPerWeek, 2), 0) / weeklyHistory.length;
  const cv = Math.sqrt(variance) / (weightedAvgPerWeek || 1);
  const confidence = cv < 0.3 ? 'high' : cv < 0.7 ? 'medium' : 'low';

  return { estimatedDate, confidence };
}
```

**Display rules:**
- Show only with ≥ 2 weeks of history and ≥ 3 sessions
- Use fuzzy language: "Finish in ~3 weeks" not "Finish by March 14"
- Show the assumption: "At your current pace of 45 min/week..."
- Cap extreme forecasts: "1+ year" → "Pick up the pace to unlock a completion estimate"
- Amber tint on card when confidence is `'low'`

---

## 5. Empty State Design

**Three states:**

**State A (zero sessions):** Illustration + "Your learning insights start here" + "Start studying" button + ghost preview of dashboard (opacity-20, pointer-events-none, aria-hidden).

**State B (1–2 sessions, < 1 week):** Show real data for what exists. Use placeholder cards for metrics that need more history (forecasting, week-over-week comparison). Include "7-Day Streak Challenge" progress widget.

**State C (7+ days of data):** Full dashboard.

**Do NOT inject sample data** — it muddy IndexedDB user data. Use ghost previews instead.

---

## 6. Chart Accessibility Guide

### ARIA Pattern

```tsx
<div role="img" aria-label="Weekly study time: 45 min Monday, 60 min Tuesday...">
  <p id="chart-desc" className="sr-only">
    Bar chart showing minutes studied each day. Highest: Tuesday at 60 min.
  </p>
  <ResponsiveContainer>
    <BarChart data={weeklyData} role="presentation" aria-hidden="true">
      ...
    </BarChart>
  </ResponsiveContainer>
</div>
```

### Accessible Data Table Alternative (WCAG AA+)

```tsx
<table id="chart-table" className="sr-only">
  <caption>Weekly Study Time</caption>
  <thead><tr><th scope="col">Day</th><th scope="col">Minutes</th></tr></thead>
  <tbody>{data.map(row => <tr key={row.date}><td>{row.date}</td><td>{row.minutes}</td></tr>)}</tbody>
</table>
```

### Colorblind-Safe Palette: Okabe-Ito

```typescript
export const CHART_PRIMARY = '#2563eb';    // blue-600 — LevelUp primary
export const CHART_SECONDARY = '#93c5fd'; // blue-300 — comparison period
export const CHART_MUTED = '#e5e7eb';     // gray-200 — empty/background bars

// Multi-series (subject distribution)
export const SUBJECT_COLORS = ['#0072B2', '#E69F00', '#009E73', '#D55E00', '#CC79A7'];
```

**Do not use red/green together.** Use blue (current) vs grey (previous) for comparison charts.

---

## 7. CSV Export Implementation

**Use `papaparse`** (~7.6 KB gzipped) — handles RFC 4180 edge cases (commas in values, newlines in strings).

```typescript
import Papa from 'papaparse';

export function exportSessionsToCSV(sessions: ExportableSession[], filename = 'levelup-study-data.csv') {
  const csv = Papa.unparse(sessions, { header: true });
  const blob = new Blob([csv], { type: 'text/csv;charset=utf-8;' });
  const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
  Object.assign(document.createElement('a'), { href: url, download: filename }).click();
  URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
}
```

**Recommended columns:** `date`, `course_name`, `subject`, `duration_minutes`, `chapters_completed`, `cumulative_minutes`, `streak_day`.

---

## 8. Dashboard Layout

### Three-Zone Structure

```
[Period Selector] [Hero KPIs: 45min | 3 days | 2 courses | 🔥5]

[Study Time Chart 60%] | [Subject Dist + Streak Heatmap 40%]

[Course Completion Table] | [Forecast Widget]
```

```tsx
<div className="space-y-6">
  <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
    <PeriodSelector value={period} onChange={setPeriod} />
    <ExportButton />
  </div>
  <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 sm:grid-cols-4">
    <KpiCard label="Study Time" value="45 min" trend="+12%" />
    <KpiCard label="Days Active" value="3 / 7" />
    <KpiCard label="Courses Active" value="2" />
    <KpiCard label="Streak" value="5 days" isHero />
  </div>
  <div className="grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-5 gap-6">
    <div className="lg:col-span-3"><StudyTimeChart period={period} /></div>
    <div className="lg:col-span-2 space-y-6">
      <SubjectDistributionChart />
      <StreakHeatmap />
    </div>
  </div>
  <div className="grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2 gap-6">
    <CourseCompletionTable />
    <ForecastWidget />
  </div>
</div>
```

**Period selector** filters all charts simultaneously via shared Zustand state (Plausible/Toggl/PostHog pattern).

**No masonry** — fixed grid that collapses gracefully. Single column mobile, 5-column desktop.

---

---

# Agent 6: AI Integration & RAG Pipeline

*Covers: Vercel AI SDK, client-side RAG, API key security, streaming UI, quiz generation, semantic search, graceful degradation, context window management*

---

## 1. Vercel AI SDK Integration Guide

### Critical Finding: Standard `useChat` Doesn't Work for SPA

`useChat` expects a backend API endpoint (`/api/chat`). For a no-backend SPA, use the raw Anthropic SDK directly.

### Recommended: `@anthropic-ai/sdk` Directly (~45 KB gzipped)

Added browser support August 2024 via `dangerouslyAllowBrowser: true` + CORS header.

```typescript
import Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk';

export function createAnthropicClient(apiKey: string): Anthropic {
  return new Anthropic({
    apiKey,
    dangerouslyAllowBrowser: true,
    defaultHeaders: { 'anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access': 'true' },
  });
}

export async function streamChatCompletion(
  client: Anthropic,
  messages: Array<{ role: 'user' | 'assistant'; content: string }>,
  systemPrompt: string,
  onChunk: (text: string) => void,
  onDone: () => void,
  signal?: AbortSignal
): Promise<void> {
  const stream = await client.messages.stream({
    model: 'claude-haiku-4-5',
    max_tokens: 1024,
    system: systemPrompt,
    messages,
  }, { signal });

  for await (const chunk of stream) {
    if (chunk.type === 'content_block_delta' && chunk.delta.type === 'text_delta') {
      onChunk(chunk.delta.text);
    }
  }
  onDone();
}
```

**Bundle comparison:**
- `@anthropic-ai/sdk`: ~45 KB gzipped ✓
- `@ai-sdk/anthropic` + `ai` (Vercel SDK): ~100 KB — unnecessary abstraction for SPA

### Model Selection

| Feature | Model | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Study assistant chat | `claude-haiku-4-5` | Fast, cheap |
| Quiz generation | `claude-sonnet-4-5` | Better structured output |
| Note summarization | `claude-haiku-4-5` | Speed matters |

---

## 2. Client-Side RAG Architecture

```
Course Content → Chunker (450 tokens, 20% overlap) → MiniSearch Index
User Query → MiniSearch.search() → Top-5 Chunks → Context Assembly
[System Prompt + Context + Conversation History + User Query] → Claude API → Stream
```

### Chunking Strategy: 400–512 tokens with 20% overlap

```typescript
const CHUNK_SIZE = 450;  // target tokens (~1800 chars English)
const CHUNK_OVERLAP = 90; // ~20% overlap

function estimateTokens(text: string): number {
  return Math.ceil(text.length / 4); // 1 token ≈ 4 chars
}
```

Split on paragraph boundaries first. Use overlap of ~2 sentences from previous chunk.

### MiniSearch Configuration

```typescript
const chunkIndex = new MiniSearch({
  fields: ['text', 'title'],
  storeFields: ['text', 'title', 'courseId', 'chapterId', 'source'],
  searchOptions: {
    boost: { title: 2, text: 1 },
    fuzzy: 0.2,
    prefix: true,
  },
});
```

### Context Assembly

```typescript
const MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS = 4000;

export function buildSystemPrompt(context: string): string {
  return `You are a helpful study assistant for an e-learning platform.
${context ? `\nRelevant course content:\n\n${context}\n\n` : ''}
Answer questions based on the provided course content when available.
If the answer is not in the course material, say so clearly rather than guessing.`;
}
```

### IndexedDB Persistence (Serialize MiniSearch Index)

```typescript
async function saveIndex(courseId: string): Promise<void> {
  await db.indexData.put({
    id: courseId,
    serialized: JSON.stringify(chunkIndex.toJSON()),
    updatedAt: new Date(),
  });
}
```

---

## 3. API Key Security Guide

### Recommendation: Session-Only Memory Storage

For a BYOK SPA, session memory is the safest approach:
- No IndexedDB/localStorage storage = no XSS extraction risk
- Lost on page refresh = user re-enters API key once per session
- Simple to implement — just Zustand store with no persistence

```typescript
export const useApiKeyStore = create<ApiKeyStore>((set, get) => ({
  apiKey: null,
  setApiKey: (key) => set({ apiKey: key.trim() }),
  clearApiKey: () => set({ apiKey: null }),
  hasKey: () => get().apiKey !== null,
}));
```

**If persistence is required:** Use Web Crypto AES-GCM with PBKDF2 key derivation from a user passphrase. Store only the cipher (never the key) in localStorage.

**Key validation on entry:**
```typescript
if (!key.startsWith('sk-ant-')) {
  alert('Invalid format. Anthropic keys start with sk-ant-');
  return;
}
```

### Critical Security Rules

1. Never log the API key — Zustand devtools can log full state. Use `devtools(store, { enabled: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' })`.
2. Never include the key in error messages.
3. Never persist to `localStorage` without encryption.
4. The key IS visible in DevTools memory inspection — this is inherent to BYOK browser apps. Document this for users.

---

## 4. Streaming Chat UI Patterns

### State Machine

```
idle → submitted → streaming → idle
                      ↓
                    error
```

### Streaming Display Components

```tsx
function StreamingMessage({ content }: { content: string }) {
  return (
    <div role="status" aria-live="polite" aria-label="AI is responding"
      className="prose prose-sm max-w-none">
      {content}
      <span className="inline-block w-0.5 h-4 bg-current ml-0.5 animate-pulse" aria-hidden="true" />
    </div>
  );
}

function ThinkingSkeleton() {
  return (
    <div role="status" aria-label="AI is thinking" className="flex gap-1 py-2">
      <span className="w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-muted-foreground/40 animate-bounce [animation-delay:0ms]" />
      <span className="w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-muted-foreground/40 animate-bounce [animation-delay:150ms]" />
      <span className="w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-muted-foreground/40 animate-bounce [animation-delay:300ms]" />
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Abort Race Condition Fix

```typescript
const abortRef = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);
const handleSubmit = async () => {
  abortRef.current?.abort(); // cancel in-flight request
  abortRef.current = new AbortController();
  await streamChat(query, abortRef.current.signal);
};
```

**`prefers-reduced-motion`:** Replace `animate-pulse` with `opacity-50` static state for the cursor.

---

## 5. AI Quiz Generation Design

### Structured Output with Zod (GA as of 2025)

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';
import { zodOutputFormat } from '@anthropic-ai/sdk/helpers/zod';

const QuizSchema = z.object({
  questions: z.array(z.object({
    question: z.string(),
    type: z.enum(['multiple-choice', 'true-false', 'short-answer']),
    options: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    correctAnswer: z.string(),
    explanation: z.string(),
    difficulty: z.enum(['beginner', 'intermediate', 'advanced']),
  })).min(1).max(10),
  topic: z.string(),
});

const response = await client.messages.parse({
  model: 'claude-sonnet-4-5',
  max_tokens: 2048,
  system: QUIZ_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
  messages: [{ role: 'user', content: `Generate ${count} questions from:\n${chapterText.slice(0, 6000)}` }],
  output_config: { format: zodOutputFormat(QuizSchema) },
});
```

**Structured Outputs are GA** for claude-haiku-4-5, claude-sonnet-4-5, claude-opus-4-5, and 4.6 variants. Guarantees valid JSON — no try/catch JSON.parse needed.

**Recursive schemas are not supported** — keep quiz schemas flat.

### Question Type Taxonomy (Bloom's Taxonomy Aligned)

| Type | Use For | Bloom's Level |
|---|---|---|
| True/False | Concept introduction | Remember |
| Multiple choice (4 options) | Key facts, definitions | Understand/Apply |
| Short answer | Definitions in own words | Understand |

Avoid open-ended questions — they require AI evaluation of student answers, adding latency.

---

## 6. Semantic Search Options

### Transformers.js — NOT Viable (Exceeds Bundle Budget)

| Component | Gzip Size |
|---|---|
| `@xenova/transformers` core | ~80 KB |
| WASM runtime | ~300 KB |
| Model weights | ~22 MB download (runtime, not bundled) |

WASM runtime alone (~300 KB) consumes 60% of the 500 KB budget. **Do not use.**

### Recommendation: Query Expansion via Claude

Add one small API call (~50 tokens) before MiniSearch retrieval:

```typescript
async function expandQuery(client: Anthropic, userQuery: string): Promise<string[]> {
  const response = await client.messages.create({
    model: 'claude-haiku-4-5',
    max_tokens: 100,
    messages: [{
      role: 'user',
      content: `Generate 3 alternative keyword search queries for this question,
one per line, no explanation:\n\n${userQuery}`,
    }],
  });
  const text = response.content[0].type === 'text' ? response.content[0].text : '';
  return [userQuery, ...text.split('\n').filter(l => l.trim()).slice(0, 3)];
}

// Search with all expanded queries, deduplicate results
```

This dramatically improves recall ("How do I read faster?" → matches "speed reading techniques") without embedding models.

---

## 7. Graceful Degradation Strategy

### Feature Gate Component

```tsx
export function AIFeatureGate({ feature, children, className }: AIFeatureGateProps) {
  const hasApiKey = useApiKeyStore(s => s.hasKey());

  if (hasApiKey) return <div className={className}>{children}</div>;

  return (
    <div className={`relative ${className}`}>
      {/* Blurred preview */}
      <div className="blur-sm pointer-events-none select-none opacity-50" aria-hidden="true">
        {children}
      </div>
      {/* Unlock overlay */}
      <div className="absolute inset-0 flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-3
        bg-background/80 rounded-[24px] p-6 text-center"
        role="region" aria-label={`${feature} requires API key`}>
        <Sparkles className="w-8 h-8 text-blue-600" aria-hidden="true" />
        <Button size="sm" variant="outline" onClick={() => {/* open API key dialog */}}>
          Add API Key to Unlock
        </Button>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

**All non-AI features work perfectly without an API key.** AI features are additive enhancements with clear affordances.

---

## 8. Context Window Management

### Token Budget (claude-haiku-4-5, 200K context)

```
System prompt:         ~500 tokens
RAG context:         ~4,000 tokens
Conversation history: ~2,000 tokens (sliding window)
User message:           ~200 tokens
Response reserved:    ~1,024 tokens
Buffer:            ~192,000 tokens (well within limits)
```

For Epic 8's use case, context window is not a meaningful constraint. Manage anyway for cost efficiency.

### Conversation Sliding Window

```typescript
const MAX_HISTORY_TOKENS = 2000;
const MAX_TURNS = 10;

export function pruneConversationHistory(
  messages: Array<{ role: string; content: string }>
): Array<{ role: string; content: string }> {
  const recent = messages.slice(-MAX_TURNS * 2);
  let totalEstimate = 0;
  const pruned: typeof messages = [];
  for (let i = recent.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
    const estimate = Math.ceil(recent[i].content.length / 4);
    if (totalEstimate + estimate > MAX_HISTORY_TOKENS) break;
    pruned.unshift(recent[i]);
    totalEstimate += estimate;
  }
  return pruned;
}
```

### Token Counting

Use the 4-chars-per-token approximation for client-side estimation. Do NOT use tiktoken — it's for OpenAI's tokenizer, not Claude's. For accurate counts, use `client.messages.countTokens()` (pre-flight API call).

---

## 9. Critical AI Gotchas

1. **Never log API keys** — Zustand devtools middleware logs full state.
2. **`requestPictureInPicture()` equivalent for AI:** API calls from `useEffect` or `setTimeout` can fail if the Anthropic API rejects browser-initiated requests from non-gesture contexts — always initiate from click handlers.
3. **Concurrent request race:** Abort previous stream before starting new one on every submit.
4. **Empty retrieval:** When MiniSearch returns 0 results, send the question to Claude WITHOUT RAG context rather than fabricating context.
5. **Chunk boundary quality:** Always split on paragraph/sentence boundaries. Mid-word splits produce useless chunks.
6. **Index staleness:** Store a content hash alongside the MiniSearch index and check on load.
7. **Rate limits (Tier 1):** claude-haiku-4-5: 50 RPM. Add a client-side rate limiter for rapid quiz generation.
8. **Bundle trap: `tiktoken`** (~150 KB gzip) — use 4-chars estimate instead.

---

---

## Summary: Key Decisions for Implementation

| Domain | Key Library Decision | Bundle Impact |
|---|---|---|
| Video transcript | `@plussub/srt-vtt-parser` OR DIY | ~3 KB or 0 KB |
| Markdown editor | `@uiw/react-md-editor` | ~65–80 KB |
| Resizable panels | `react-resizable-panels` (via shadcn/ui) | ~6–8 KB |
| Autosave | `use-debounce` | ~1 KB |
| PDF export | `react-to-print` + `window.print()` | ~3 KB |
| Heatmap | `react-activity-calendar` v3 | ~25–30 KB |
| Confetti | `canvas-confetti` | ~4 KB |
| CSV export | `papaparse` (core, not react wrapper) | ~7.6 KB |
| AI SDK | `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (direct, not Vercel AI SDK) | ~45 KB |
| Spaced repetition | Custom SM-2 in TypeScript | 0 KB |
| Semantic search | MiniSearch + Claude query expansion | 0 additional KB |
| Transformers.js | ❌ DISQUALIFIED (300 KB WASM alone) | N/A |
| `@react-pdf/renderer` | ❌ DISQUALIFIED (392 KB alone) | N/A |

**Estimated new bundle additions (Epics 3–8):**
- ~65–80 KB: `@uiw/react-md-editor`
- ~45 KB: `@anthropic-ai/sdk`
- ~30 KB: `react-activity-calendar`
- ~20 KB: misc (canvas-confetti, react-to-print, use-debounce, papaparse)
- **Total additional: ~160–175 KB gzipped** — well within 500 KB budget given existing stack.
